title: storage notation (persistence contract)
manager: A notation for declaring where a machine's durable state lives — the storage-binding syntax that names the persistence backend (which store, what retention, how often to snapshot) as part of the machine or its deployment, rather than wiring it into host code. This is the source-level face of the persistence contract: a machine can declare 'my snapshots go here, my tape ring is this long' so durability becomes configuration a reader can see, not a bespoke integration everyone reimplements slightly differently.
cs: storage/persistence notation binding a machine to the pluggable store API (W9.1); declares store, snapshot cadence, retention; the source-level face of the persistence contract.
